pages.text: Home | eAnudaan Maintenance Activity Notice The e-Anudaan portal will be undergoing scheduled maintenance from 09:00 am on 10th May 2025 to 11:30 pm on 12th May 2025 . During this period, the e-Anudaan portal will not be accessible. Close Note: All NGOs are requested to submit applications for the release of the 2nd and 3rd installments for the Financial Years 2024-25 and 2025-26 respectively, in order to obtain the remaining 20% grant-in-aid for FY 2024-25 and 40% as the second installment for FY 2025-26. ग़ैर सरकारी संगठनों से अनुरोध है कि वे वित्तीय वर्ष 2024–25 के शेष 20 प्रतिशत अनुदान की भुगतान हेतु तथा वित्तीय वर्ष 2025–26 की द्वितीय किस्त के रूप में 40 प्रतिशत अनुदान प्राप्त करने के लिए, क्रमशः द्वितीय और तृतीय किस्तों के जारी किए जाने हेतु आवेदन प्रस्तुत करें। Online Portal for Grant-in-Aid Schemes The portal facilitates online submission of NGO’s proposals alongwith the prescribed documents and automates the complete process for issuance of Grant-in-Aid. Various Back office user have privilege for application processing as per the role defined at District, State Directorate, State Secretariat and at the Central Ministry. The process involves the application verification, checklist filling, inspection report, scrutinizing, screening, recommendation, financial approval and GIA sanction. newspaper New/Current All projects under the IPSrC Scheme are required to mandatorily furnish the requisite details in the prescribed proforma and submit the same at mosje.src1@gmail.com.: 29-05-2026 Uploading of Proforma on e-Anudaan Portal for Collection of CCTV Infrastructure Details. Common registration details of organisation available on NGO-Darpan will automatically be auto-filled in non-editing mode. A few details like Username/ Password and scheme etc. are required to be submitted by NGO. Take care of all input fields marked with * which are mandatory to fill and follow the instructions. * Registration on this portal needs to be done only once. The NGO(s) who had registered earlier on this portal need not to register again. NGO(s) who have registered multiple times and are having multiple login/userid accounts, will be shown all login accounts. NGO would select only one login/user id account to keep it active in future. All other login/user id account(s) will be deactivated however would remain available for uploading the deficient documents. * For updating any NGO registration details at e-Anudaan, it is to be updated first at NGO Darpan. Update Profile option in NGO login at e-Anudaan would enable NGO to update the profile details. Department of Social Justice and Empowerment

*****
 Shastri Bhawan, New Delhi
        
the         01st July, 2024
 
NOTICE INVITING APPLICATIONS
 
            Applications are invited from the eligible
NGOs/Trusts/Charitable institutions etc. on this Ministry’s e-
anudaan portal (https://grants-msje.gov.in/) for new projects
of  i.) Senior Citizen Home for 25 beneﬁciaries (SrCH-25)
(all genders),  ii) Senior Citizen Home for 50 Beneﬁciaries
(SrCH-50) (all genders) and iii.) Continuous Care Home for
persons aﬄicted with Alzheimer's/Dementia (CCH) for 20
beneﬁciaries (all genders), for the year 2024-25  under the
Integrated Programme for Senior Citizens (IPSrC), a sub-
scheme of Atal Vayo Abhyuday Yojana (AVYAY) in the Gap
districts across various States/ Union Territories. Tentative
list of such Gap districts is at 
Annexure
.
 
2.         The detailed eligibility criteria, quantum and
conditions of assistance etc. have been described in AVYAY
scheme guideline which has already been uploaded on the
website of the Ministry
(https://socialjustice.gov.in/writereaddata/UploadFile/634916
59437974.pdf). Cost norms of the projects are given at page
45 to 47 of the Scheme Guideline.
 
3.       Applicant organisations are requested to kindly go
through diﬀerent provisions of the scheme guideline
thoroughly before applying. The Organisation /
Implementing agency should have been registered for at
least two years, and should have had at least two years'
experience in the social sector at the time of applying for
grant under the scheme.
 
4.       Eligible organisations are required to apply through e-
Anudaan portal ONLY (https://grants-msje.gov.in/ngo-login).
Before apply, the organisation is requested to kindly check
AG-15039/37/2021-Sr.C-I
I/40304/2024
with District Administration whether the district for which
the project is being proposed is a GAP district or not. No
other means of application shall be entertained. 
Department of
Social Justice and Empowerment (DoSJE) reserves the right to cancel this
notice, and/or invite afresh proposals with or without amendments to this
document without liability or any obligation for such document, and without
assigning any reason. DoSJE reserves the right to take ﬁnal decision
regarding selection of implementing agency or project.
 
5.       For any queries Organisations can contact at
bharati.gusain@nic.in
 with the subject ‘
Query regarding
new project under IPSrC
’.
 
 
6.       The applications from the organisations from the gap
Districts shall be accepted from 01st July 2024 till 15st
August, 2024.
 
 
 
(Bharati Gusain)
Under Secretary to the Government of India
e-Mail: 
bharati.gusain@nic.in

NIC division with a request to open the portal for New
projects for FY 2024-25.

NOTICE INVITING APPLICATIONS
            Applications are invited from the eligible organisations on
this Ministry’s e-anudaan portal (https://grants-msje.gov.in/) for
new Regional Resource and Training Centres (RRTCs) for the year
2024-25  under the Integrated Programme for Senior Citizens
(IPSrC), a sub-scheme of Atal Vayo Abhyuday Yojana. (AVYAY),
from all States/UTs 
except
 Delhi, Himachal Pradesh, Karnataka,
Maharashtra, Manipur, Mizoram, Odisha, Tamil Nadu, Telangana
and West Bengal.
2.         Role of RRTCs is to act as key nodal agencies on aged care
under overall direction & supervision of Ministry of Social Justice
and Empowerment, providing overall technical support and inputs
on senior citizens programs in their assigned States allocated by
the Ministry. The RRTCs will spread across the geographical
regions of the country, covering all the States/UTs. RRTCs carry
out advocacy, awareness generation, training of stakeholders,
database generation, inspections, monitoring, research and liaising
with the State Governments of the States assigned in the ﬁeld of
old age care. The RRTCs will be agencies through which the
Ministry and National Institute of Social Defence (NISD) reach out
to the end beneficiary senior citizens.
3.         The detailed eligibility criteria, quantum and conditions of
assistance etc. have been described in AVYAY scheme guidelines
which has already been uploaded on the website of the Ministry
(https://socialjustice.gov.in/) and on e-Anudaan Portal
(
https://grants-msje.gov.in/ngo-login
). Matrix for eligibility Criteria
of RRTCs and Parameters are attached at Enclosures (1&2). The
applications from the Organisations for  the above- mentioned
States/UTs shall be accepted from 01st July 2024 till 31st July,
2024.
Yours faithfully,

Research Associate

ethical considerations in collaboration 
with relevant stakeholders, including 
senior citizen advocacy groups,

academic institutions.

appropriate methodologies to generate 
insights into the needs and 
experiences of older adults.

communicate results through written

c) Ability to communicate effectively

Roles and Responsibilities

Government of India

Department of Social Justice and Empowerment
(Senior Citizen Division)
*****
 Shastri Bhawan, New Delhi
        
the         01st July, 2024
 
NOTICE INVITING APPLICATIONS
 
            Applications are invited from the eligible
NGOs/Trusts/Charitable institutions etc. on this Ministry’s e-
anudaan portal (https://grants-msje.gov.in/) for new projects
of  i.) Senior Citizen Home for 25 beneﬁciaries (SrCH-25)
(all genders),  ii) Senior Citizen Home for 50 Beneﬁciaries
(SrCH-50) (all genders) and iii.) Continuous Care Home for
persons aﬄicted with Alzheimer's/Dementia (CCH) for 20
beneﬁciaries (all genders), for the year 2024-25  under the
Integrated Programme for Senior Citizens (IPSrC), a sub-
scheme of Atal Vayo Abhyuday Yojana (AVYAY) in the Gap
districts across various States/ Union Territories. Tentative
list of such Gap districts is at 
Annexure
.
 
2.         The detailed eligibility criteria, quantum and
conditions of assistance etc. have been described in AVYAY
scheme guideline which has already been uploaded on the
website of the Ministry
(https://socialjustice.gov.in/writereaddata/UploadFile/634916
59437974.pdf). Cost norms of the projects are given at page
45 to 47 of the Scheme Guideline.
 
3.       Applicant organisations are requested to kindly go
through diﬀerent provisions of the scheme guideline
thoroughly before applying. The Organisation /
Implementing agency should have been registered for at
least two years, and should have had at least two years'
experience in the social sector at the time of applying for
grant under the scheme.
 
4.       Eligible organisations are required to apply through e-
Anudaan portal ONLY (https://grants-msje.gov.in/ngo-login).
Before apply, the organisation is requested to kindly check
AG-15039/37/2021-Sr.C-I
I/40304/2024
with District Administration whether the district for which
the project is being proposed is a GAP district or not. No
other means of application shall be entertained. 
Department of
Social Justice and Empowerment (DoSJE) reserves the right to cancel this
notice, and/or invite afresh proposals with or without amendments to this
document without liability or any obligation for such document, and without
assigning any reason. DoSJE reserves the right to take ﬁnal decision
regarding selection of implementing agency or project.
 
5.       For any queries Organisations can contact at
bharati.gusain@nic.in
 with the subject ‘
Query regarding
new project under IPSrC
’.
 
 
6.       The applications from the organisations from the gap
Districts shall be accepted from 01st July 2024 till 15st
August, 2024.
 
 
 
(Bharati Gusain)
Under Secretary to the Government of India
e-Mail: 
bharati.gusain@nic.in

NIC division with a request to open the portal for New
projects for FY 2024-25.

NOTICE INVITING APPLICATIONS
            Applications are invited from the eligible organisations on
this Ministry’s e-anudaan portal (https://grants-msje.gov.in/) for
new Regional Resource and Training Centres (RRTCs) for the year
2024-25  under the Integrated Programme for Senior Citizens
(IPSrC), a sub-scheme of Atal Vayo Abhyuday Yojana. (AVYAY),
from all States/UTs 
except
 Delhi, Himachal Pradesh, Karnataka,
Maharashtra, Manipur, Mizoram, Odisha, Tamil Nadu, Telangana
and West Bengal.
2.         Role of RRTCs is to act as key nodal agencies on aged care
under overall direction & supervision of Ministry of Social Justice
and Empowerment, providing overall technical support and inputs
on senior citizens programs in their assigned States allocated by
the Ministry. The RRTCs will spread across the geographical
regions of the country, covering all the States/UTs. RRTCs carry
out advocacy, awareness generation, training of stakeholders,
database generation, inspections, monitoring, research and liaising
with the State Governments of the States assigned in the ﬁeld of
old age care. The RRTCs will be agencies through which the
Ministry and National Institute of Social Defence (NISD) reach out
to the end beneficiary senior citizens.
3.         The detailed eligibility criteria, quantum and conditions of
assistance etc. have been described in AVYAY scheme guidelines
which has already been uploaded on the website of the Ministry
(https://socialjustice.gov.in/) and on e-Anudaan Portal
(
https://grants-msje.gov.in/ngo-login
). Matrix for eligibility Criteria
of RRTCs and Parameters are attached at Enclosures (1&2). The
applications from the Organisations for  the above- mentioned
States/UTs shall be accepted from 01st July 2024 till 31st July,
2024.

analysis.overview: The National Action Plan for Drug Demand Reduction (NAPDDR) is a centrally sponsored scheme under the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment aimed at reducing drug demand through preventive education, awareness generation, counseling, treatment, rehabilitation, and skill development for affected individuals. It supports NGOs and other eligible organizations in setting up and running District De-Addiction Centres (DDACs), Garima Greh (shelters for transgender persons), and other related interventions under its sub-schemes like SMILE and AVYAY. The scheme operates through the e-Anudaan portal for online submission of proposals, document verification, and grant-in-aid disbursement. It targets vulnerable populations including persons affected by substance abuse, transgender individuals, and senior citizens in need of care and protection.
objectives: Preventive education and awareness generation against alcoholism and substance (drugs) abuse
Counseling, treatment, and rehabilitation of drug-dependent persons
Capacity building and skill development of beneficiaries under PM-DAKSH programme
Support for setting up and running District De-Addiction Centres (DDACs)
Support for Garima Greh (shelters for transgender persons) under SMILE scheme
Support for Senior Citizen Homes and Continuous Care Homes under AVYAY/IPSrC
Geo-tagging of ministry-supported institutions for better accessibility
analysis.eligibility: Eligible organisations include NGOs, Trusts, and Charitable institutions registered for at least two years with a minimum of two years' experience in the social sector at the time of applying. They must register on the NGO-DARPAN portal of NITI Aayog to obtain a NGO Unique ID before applying on the e-Anudaan portal. For specific sub-schemes like IPSrC under AVYAY, applicants must propose projects in designated Gap districts as per the tentative list annexed in the guidelines. Applications must be submitted exclusively through the e-Anudaan portal (https://grants-msje.gov.in/ngo-login) with complete documentation.
analysis.benefits: Financial support in the form of Grant-in-Aid (GIA) for setting up and running projects such as DDACs, Garima Greh, Senior Citizen Homes, and Continuous Care Homes. Non-financial benefits include technical support, guidance on scheme guidelines, access to training under PM-DAKSH for beneficiaries, geo-tagging of facilities for better service accessibility, and facilitation of monitoring and inspection processes via the e-Anudaan portal. The portal automates application verification, checklist filling, inspection report scrutiny, screening, recommendation, financial approval, and GIA sanction.
analysis.financial_support: Financial assistance is provided as Grant-in-Aid (GIA) to eligible NGOs for recurring and non-recurring expenses related to the establishment and operation of projects under NAPDDR, including DDACs, Garima Greh, Senior Citizen Homes, and Continuous Care Homes. The quantum of assistance, cost norms, and conditions are detailed in the respective scheme guidelines (e.g., AVYAY scheme guideline) uploaded on the Ministry's website and the e-Anudaan portal. Funds are disbursed in installments (e.g., 1st, 2nd, 3rd/final) based on project progress and submission of required documents and inspection reports.
analysis.application_process: 1. Register on the NGO-DARPAN portal (https://ngodarpan.gov.in) to obtain a NGO Unique ID. 2. Access the e-Anudaan portal at https://grants-msje.gov.in/ngo-login. 3. Log in using credentials and ensure NGO Unique ID and PAN are submitted during registration. 4. Navigate to the relevant scheme (e.g., NAPDDR, DDAC, Garima Greh, AVYAY/IPSrC) under 'Notice Inviting Applications'. 5. Fill in the application form with project details. 6. Upload prescribed documents including NGO Unique ID, PAN, project proposal, and other scheme-specific documents. 7. Submit the application before the deadline. 8. Await verification, inspection, and approval by district, state, and central authorities. 9. Upon approval, Grant-in-Aid will be sanctioned and disbursed in installments.
required_documents: NGO Unique ID from NGO-DARPAN portal
UDID number or UDID enrollment ID with disability certificate (for DDRS scheme from FY 2023-24)
analysis.deadlines: Varies by scheme and notice; e.g., applications for new projects under IPSrC/AVYAY in Gap districts were accepted from 01st July 2024 till 15th August 2024 (as per one notice), while RRTC applications were accepted from 01st July 2024 till 31st July 2024. Deadlines are specified in individual notices; applicants must check the latest notice on the e-Anudaan portal.
analysis.implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
analysis.contact_details: Email: bharati.gusain@nic.in (for IPSrC/AVYAY queries), support[dot]ngo-msje[at]nic[dot]in (technical support), feedbackddrs[at]gmail[dot]com (DDRS scheme feedback)
caveats: Applications must be submitted exclusively through the e-Anudaan portal; no other means of application shall be entertained.
For DDRS scheme from FY 2023-24, UDID number or UDID enrollment ID with disability certificate is mandatory; no grants will be released without it.
NGOs must first register on NGO-DARPAN portal and obtain a NGO Unique ID before applying on e-Anudaan.
The Ministry reserves the right to cancel notices, invite afresh proposals, or take final decisions on selection without assigning any reason.
Project proposals must conform to the prescribed proforma and scheme guidelines.
Some schemes are restricted to specific districts (e.g., Gap districts for AVYAY/IPSrC).
